sect1.title: faac-&faac-version;

sect2.title: Installation of faac
sect2.para: Install faac by running the following commands:

screen.userinput:
mkdir build && cd build &&
meson setup .. \ --prefix=/usr \ --buildtype=release \ -D default_library=shared &&
ninja

sect2.para: This package does not come with a test suite. However, basic functionality can be tested by encoding a sample WAV file (the sample file is installed by the package:
Then, decode the result using the faad program from the package and play back the decoded file (requires the aplay program from the package:
aplay should identify the file as Signed 16 bit Little Endian, Rate 48000 Hz, Stereo, and you should hear the words front left.

sect2.title: Command Explanations
sect2.para: -D default_library=shared: prevents building the static library, which is not needed on a BLFS system.

sect2.title: Other AAC encoders
sect2.para: The quality of faac is not up to par with the best AAC encoders currently available. Also, it only supports AAC and not High Efficiency AAC (also known as aacPlus), which provides better quality at low bitrates by means of using the spectral band replication technology. One example of an alternative program for producing AAC and HE-AAC streams is:

listitem.para: 3GPP Enhanced aacPlus general audio codec: available in the source form, can encode only HE-AAC up to 48 kbps out of the box, but the maximum bitrate can be changed by editing the tuning table in the FloatFR_sbrenclib/src/sbr_main.c file.

sect2.para: Note, however, that the iPod supports only the Low Complexity AAC profile, which is the default in faac, but is completely unavailable in the 3GPP encoder.

listitem.para: contains functions for encoding AAC streams
contains functions for creating and manipulating MP4 files
